tool.name: Genetic format conversion
tool.description: using PGDSpider and additional conversion modules.
tool.command:
mkdir -p convert_directory && mkdir -p pop_affiliation &&
bash '$__tool_directory__/genetic_format_conversion.sh' '${input_file}' '${input_file.element_identifier}' '$data_conditional.input_format' '$data_conditional.output_selection.output_format'
#if $data_conditional.input_format == "vcf" and $data_conditional.output_selection.output_format == "genepop": '$data_conditional.output_selection.ploidy' '$data_conditional.output_selection.pl_gl' '$data_conditional.output_selection.exclude_loci' '$data_conditional.output_selection.non_polymorphic' '$data_conditional.output_selection.indels'
#if $data_conditional.output_selection.input_regions: '$data_conditional.output_selection.input_regions' #else: '' #end if
#if str($data_conditional.output_selection.phred_scaled) != '': '$data_conditional.output_selection.phred_scaled' #else: '' #end if
#if str($data_conditional.output_selection.missing_genotype_quality) != '': '$data_conditional.output_selection.missing_genotype_quality' #else: '' #end if
#if str($data_conditional.output_selection.missing_read_depth) != '': '$data_conditional.output_selection.missing_read_depth' #else: '' #end if
#if $data_conditional.output_selection.individuals: '$data_conditional.output_selection.individuals' #else: '' #end if
'$data_conditional.output_selection.indpop_file.indpop_inc' #if $data_conditional.output_selection.indpop_file.indpop_inc == "true": '$data_conditional.output_selection.indpop_file.indpop' #else: '' #end if
#elif $data_conditional.input_format == "tabular" and $data_conditional.output_selection.output_format == "genepop": '$data_conditional.output_selection.sep_char' '$data_conditional.output_selection.digit_num'
#elif $data_conditional.input_format == "genepop" and $data_conditional.output_selection.output_format == "bayescan": '$data_conditional.output_selection.indpop' '$data_conditional.output_selection.marker_type' '$data_conditional.output_selection.alleles_coded'
#end if

param.name: input_file
param.label: Upload your input file

param.name: input_format
param.label: Data input file format

param.name: output_format
param.label: Input format

param.name: ploidy
param.label: What is the ploidy of the data?

param.name: input_regions
param.label: Only input following regions (refSeqName:stard:end, multiple regions: whitespace separated):

param.name: pl_gl
param.label: Take most likely genotype if 'PL' or 'GL' is given in the genotype field?

param.name: phred_scaled
param.label: Only output SNPs with a phred-scaled quality of at least:

param.name: missing_genotype_quality
param.label: Output genotypes as missing if the phred-scale genotype quality is below:

param.name: missing_read_depth
param.label: Output genotypes as missing if the read depth of a position for the sample is below:

param.name: exclude_loci
param.label: Do you want to exclude loci with only missing data?

param.name: individuals
param.label: Only output following individuals (ind1, ind2, ind4, ...):

param.name: non_polymorphic
param.label: Do you want to include non-polymorphic SNPs?

param.name: indels
param.label: Do you want to include INDELS as STANDARD genetic markers?

param.name: indpop_inc
param.label: Do you want to include a file with population definitions?

param.name: indpop
param.label: Upload your population definitions

param.name: output_format
param.label: Output format

param.name: digit_num
param.label: Number of digits per allele
param.help: Enter the number of digits used to represent each allele in your input file. For example, if your microsatellite alleles are coded like 192, 168, 087, then each allele is 3 digits long, enter 3.

param.name: output_format
param.label: Output format

param.name: marker_type
param.label: Select the type of data

param.name: indpop
param.label: Upload your population file
param.help: File containing individuals in the first column and their population in the second, separated by a tabulation character.

param.name: alleles_coded
param.label: How are Microsat alleles coded?

option.value: as the length of the PCR fragments

param.option: as the length of the PCR fragments

param.name: data_conditional|output_selection|alleles_coded
param.value: as the length of the PCR fragments

param.name: data_conditional|output_selection|alleles_coded
param.value: as the length of the PCR fragments

tool.help:
Usage ===== As input, users provide a file in one of three accepted formats: VCF (Danecek *et al.* 2011), GENEPOP (Raymond & Rousset 1995) or Tabular (see **SSR Genotype file** format below). After selecting the desired output format, users can configure formatting paramters, most of which pre-set default values.
The tool supports three primary conversion :
1. **VCF → GENEPOP**: The tool uses PGDSpider (Lischer & Excoffier 2012) to parse variant call data. It generates a GENEPOP file containing the list of loci followed by individual genotypes, formatted for use in population genetics software and R packages. Users must provide a population assignment file to define population groupings and specify ploidy as well as filtering options (minimum phred-scaled quality, minimum genotype quality, minimum read depth, INDEL inclusion, etc.).
2. **Tabular → GENEPOP**: SSR genotype data provided in tabular format are converted into GENEPOP format directly (without PGDSpider). The user specifies the allele separator and the number of digits used to encode alleles. The tool parses individual genotypes per population and writes a standard GENEPOP file.
3. **GENEPOP → Geste/BayeScan**: The tool uses PGDSpider to restructure genotype data into allele count matrices formatted according to the BayeScan specification (Foll & Gaggiotti 2008). Users provide a population assignment file to define the population order in the output. The output file declares the number of loci and populations, followed by population-specific allele counts per locus, ensuring compatibility with Bayesian F\ :sub:`ST`\-based inference analyses. A `pop_affiliation` file is generated, containing the name of the population and the number corresponding to its order of appearance in the Bayes output.
NB: PGDSpider is a powerful automated data conversion tool for population genetics and genomics (https://software.bioinformatics.unibe.ch/pgdspider/). It is used here for VCF → GENEPOP and GENEPOP → Geste/BayeScan conversions.
**SSR Genotype file (tab):**
::
Ind Pop Loc1 Loc2 Loc3 ind1 pop1 120/124 200/202 150/150 ind2 pop1 120/120 202/204 150/152 ind3 pop2 124/126 200/200 152/152
**Genepop file (txt or tabular) :**
::
loc1 loc2 . . . locX POP ind1, 0101 0101 ... ind2, 0001 0101 ... POP ind3, 0100 0101 ... ind4, 0100 0101 ...
This file can be used as input for the LDNe software to estimate the effective population size.
**GESTE/ BayeScan file (txt or tabular):**
::
[loci]=46
[populations]=6
[pop]=1 1 106 4 0 52 54 0 2 104 3 17 86 1 3 102 8 0 4 0 25 67 4 2 0
This file can be used as input for the BayeScan tool.
**Population assignement file (tab-separated)**
::
ind1 pop1 ind2 pop2 ind3 pop2 ind4 pop1 ind5 pop3
**Pop affiliation order (tab-separated)**
::
pop1 1 pop2 2
See also examples of VCF formats in : https://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/VCFv4.5.pdf
